name: King Salman Mosque
description: The largest mosque in the Maldives, with a capacity for 10,000 worshippers. Its iconic blue roof and five minarets, representing the five pillars of Islam, dominate the Malé skyline. It also houses a library and classrooms.

name: Islamic Centre (Masjid-al-Sultan Muhammad Thakurufaanu Al Auzam)
description: A landmark in the capital, known for its grand golden dome and towering minaret. It serves as the main hub for Islamic affairs in the country.

name: Malé Friday Mosque (Hukuru Miskiy)
description: The oldest mosque in Malé, a UNESCO tentative site famous for its intricate coral stone architecture and detailed lacquer work.

name: Masjid Al-Noor
description: A well-known mosque in the Maafannu district of Malé, offering a peaceful prayer space and dedicated ladies' area.

name: Eid Mosque (Eid Miskih)
description: A historic coral stone mosque built in the early 19th century, primarily used for Eid prayers and known for its distinctive minaret.

name: Masjidhul Al-Sulthan Muhammad Ibn Abdhullah
description: A modern, multi-story mosque located on Ameenee Magu, serving a large congregation in the heart of the city.

name: Bihuroazu Kamanaa Mosque
description: A historic mosque with traditional coral stone walls and intricate carvings, located near the tomb of Sultan Muhammad Thakurufaanu. It reflects strong Persian architectural influences.

name: Masjid Al-Furqan
description: A key neighborhood mosque in the Galolhu district, known for its active community and educational programs.

name: Masjid Al-Jalaaluddin
description: A local mosque serving the Machchangolhi ward of Malé, providing daily prayer services and religious classes.

name: Hulhumalé Mosque
description: The primary mosque on the reclaimed island of Hulhumalé, featuring modern Islamic architecture and serving the island's rapidly growing population.

name: Villimalé Friday Mosque
description: The central mosque for the island of Villimalé, known for its strong community spirit and beautiful seaside location.

name: Shangri-La Mosque
description: A prominent mosque in Addu Atoll, named after the nearby Shangri-La resort, serving the local community of Feydhoo.

name: Gan Island Mosque
description: The main mosque on Gan Island, historically significant due to the former Royal Air Force base. It serves as a spiritual center for the local population.

name: Thinadhoo Friday Mosque
description: The main mosque for Thinadhoo, the capital of Gaafu Dhaalu Atoll. A large, modern structure that can accommodate worshippers from across the island.

name: Fenfushi Friday Mosque
description: A UNESCO tentative site and one of the country's few remaining coral stone mosques. Its well-preserved state offers a glimpse into 17th-century Maldivian craftsmanship.

name: Baarah Friday Mosque
description: The main mosque on the historic island of Baarah, a key center for Friday prayers in the northernmost atoll of the Maldives.

name: Kulhudhuffushi Friday Mosque
description: One of the largest mosques in the north of Maldives, serving Kulhudhuffushi, the economic capital of the northern atolls.

name: Fuvahmulah Friday Mosque
description: The grand mosque of Fuvahmulah, a single-island atoll. It's a central landmark for the island's unique community and culture.

name: Al Yoosuf Mosque
description: A historically significant mosque in Baa Atoll, named Al Yoosuf. It has been a center for Islamic learning for decades.

name: Mahibadhoo Friday Mosque
description: The central mosque for Mahibadhoo, the capital island of Alif Dhaal Atoll, known for its welcoming congregation.
